name: AI Recruiter Agent
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Recruitment intelligence — GPT-4o for resume analysis and candidate-role matching (extracting skills, experience, and qualifications from unstructured resumes and mapping to job requirements), job description generation from hiring manager briefs, interview question generation tailored to role and seniority, candidate communication drafting, and bias-aware evaluation summaries; GPT-4o-mini for high-volume resume screening and FAQ responses

dev.note: ~60K tokens/day, resume parsing and matching prototype

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Candidate search and talent pool — hybrid search across candidate profiles combining keyword matching (job titles, certifications, company names) with vector similarity for semantic skill matching ('machine learning experience' matches candidates with 'PyTorch, TensorFlow, neural networks' even without exact keyword overlap); faceted filtering by location, experience level, availability, and skill category

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Recruitment operations data — candidate application records, interview schedules and feedback, hiring pipeline stages (applied → screened → interviewed → offered → hired), recruiter activity logs, job requisition details, offer tracking, and onboarding status with low-latency reads for real-time recruitment dashboard

services.name: Microsoft Graph
services.purpose: Calendar and email integration — interview scheduling via Outlook calendar API (find available slots across interviewers, send invites, handle rescheduling), candidate email communication, Teams meeting creation for virtual interviews, organizational hierarchy for hiring manager approval workflows, and SharePoint integration for job posting distribution

dev.note: Graph API calls within M365 license

prod.note: Graph API calls within M365 license

enterprise.note: Graph API calls within M365 license

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Document storage — candidate resumes (PDF, DOCX), cover letters, portfolio documents, interview recordings (with consent), job description templates, offer letter templates, and compliance documentation for audit retention

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, AI Search keys, Cosmos DB connection strings, Graph API client secrets, ATS integration credentials, and background check vendor API keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Recruitment platform observability — candidate matching accuracy, resume processing latency, pipeline conversion rates, bias detection metrics (demographic distribution at each pipeline stage), recruiter productivity analytics, and time-to-hire tracking

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for initial resume screening (pass/fail against minimum qualifications) and reserve GPT-4o for detailed candidate-role matching analysis — 70% of applications are clearly unqualified and can be filtered with the mini model at 1/30th the cost

Index candidate profiles in AI Search with incremental updates — only re-embed profiles when skills or experience change, not on every status update (interview scheduled, note added), reducing embedding generation costs by 80%
Cache AI-generated job descriptions and interview questions by role template — similar positions (e.g., 'Senior Software Engineer') share 80% of content, reducing OpenAI calls for repeat requisitions
Use Azure AI Search Free tier for dev (50MB, ~1K profiles) — sufficient for prototype development without any search service cost

Implement candidate data lifecycle policies — archive candidate records to cool storage 1 year after position closure (legal retention requirement), delete after the compliance-required retention period, reducing hot storage costs

Batch resume processing during off-peak hours for bulk uploads (job fair imports, ATS migrations) — reduces Function App scaling spikes and allows more efficient token usage with GPT-4o-mini batch API
